stem: In a right triangle, one acute angle has measure x°. The length of the hypotenuse is 20, and the length of the leg adjacent to the angle with measure x° is 7. What is the value of cos x°? (Note: figure not drawn to scale.)
rationale: The cosine of an acute angle in a right triangle is the ratio of the adjacent leg to the hypotenuse: cos x° = 7/20 = 0.35.
